info.name: Network
info.comment: Provides miscellaneous network functions and hosts the NetSocket, NetServer and ClientSocket classes.

description.p: The Network module exports a few miscellaneous networking functions. For core network functionality surrounding sockets and HTTP, please refer to the NetSocket, NetServer and HTTP classes.

function.name: AddressToStr
function.comment: Converts an IPAddress structure to an IPAddress in dotted string form.

input.param: A pointer to the IPAddress structure.

description.p: Converts an IPAddress structure to a string containing the IPAddress in dotted format. Please free the resulting string with FreeResource once it is no longer required.

function.result: The IP address is returned as an allocated string.

function.name: HostToLong
function.comment: Converts a 32 bit (unsigned) long from host to network byte order.

input.param: Data in host byte order to be converted to network byte order

description.p: Converts a 32 bit (unsigned) long from host to network byte order.

function.result: The long in network byte order

function.name: HostToShort
function.comment: Converts a 16 bit (unsigned) word from host to network byte order.

input.param: Data in host byte order to be converted to network byte order

description.p: Converts a 16 bit (unsigned) word from host to network byte order.

function.result: The word in network byte order

function.name: LongToHost
function.comment: Converts a 32 bit (unsigned) long from network to host byte order.

input.param: Data in network byte order to be converted to host byte order

description.p: Converts a 32 bit (unsigned) long from network to host byte order.

function.result: The Value in host byte order.

function.name: SetSSL
function.comment: Alters SSL settings on an initialised NetSocket client object.
function.prototype: ERR net::SetSSL(objNetSocket * NetSocket, const std::string_view & Command, const std::string_view & Value)

input.param: Name of a command or option to set (case-sensitive, camel-case).
Value to set for the command or option.

description.p: Use the SetSSL() function to adjust the SSL capabilities of a NetSocket client object. Server-side SSL is configured on NetServer before initialisation with the SSL flag and certificate fields. The following commands are currently available:

list.li: EnableSSL: Starts an SSL handshaking process with the remote server. Does nothing if the socket is already in SSL mode.
DisableSSL: Disconnects the SSL connection and reverts to unencrypted mode.

description.p: If a failure occurs when executing a command, the execution of all further commands is aborted and the error code is returned immediately.
SetSSL() can also be used to check if SSL is supported in the current build, in which case ERR::NoSecureSockets will be the return value if all other arguments are NULL.

result.error: The NetSocket argument was not specified.
SSL support is disabled in this build.

function.name: ShortToHost
function.comment: Converts a 16 bit (unsigned) word from network to host byte order.

input.param: Data in network byte order to be converted to host byte order

description.p: Converts a 16 bit (unsigned) word from network to host byte order.

function.result: The Value in host byte order

function.name: StrToAddress
function.comment: Converts an IP Address in string form to an IPAddress structure.
function.prototype: ERR net::StrToAddress(const std::string_view & String, struct IPAddress * Address)

input.param: A null-terminated string containing the IP Address in dotted format.
Must point to an IPAddress structure that will be filled in.

description.p: Converts an IPv4 or an IPv6 address in string format to an IPAddress structure. The String must be of form 1.2.3.4 (IPv4) or 2001:db8::1 (IPv6). IPv6 addresses are automatically detected by the presence of colons.

result.error: The String was not a valid IP Address.

constants.comment: Address types for the IPAddress structure.

constants.const: Contact the name service and do not use the local DNS cache.
Disable SSL server certificate verification for client sockets (testing only).
Enable TCP keep-alive probes using operating system defaults.
Allow multiple connections from the same IP (NetServer only).
Use Secure Sockets Layer for all communication.
Use UDP (connectionless datagram protocol) instead of TCP.
There is an active connection at present.
An SSL connection is being established.
A NetServer reports MULTISTATE because accepted ClientSocket objects track their own states.
The host name is being resolved.

constants.comment: These error codes for certificate validation match the OpenSSL error codes (X509 definitions)

struct.name: IPAddress
struct.field: 128-bit array for supporting both V4 (32-bit host order) and V6 (8-bit byte order) IP addresses.
Identifies the address Data value as a V4 or V6 address type.
For UDP packets, identifies the client port number in host byte order.
